art-106080c3c9f4.title: The Government's Warning Shot? FDA and HHS Turn Up the Pressure on Compounding
art-106080c3c9f4.summary: Hyman, Phelps and McNamara analysis of whether compounders are on notice after FDA's February 2026 enforcement escalation. The article examines whether the FDA may be preparing to pursue at least one compounder through the Department of Justice, particularly after the HHS General Counsel's referral of Hims to the DOJ.

art-2e40fcca311a.title: FDA Tightens the Belt on GLP-1 Compounding, Escalating Threat of Enforcement
art-2e40fcca311a.summary: Analysis of FDA's escalating enforcement posture. High-volume, standardized formulation, and national distribution are no longer being treated as aggressive compounding but as manufacturing. 503A pharmacies must remain patient-specific in both form and reality.

art-2331b4279797.title: Does GLP-1 Compounding Foretell Patent Enforcement Outside the Hatch-Waxman Framework?
art-2331b4279797.summary: Morgan Lewis analysis of how patent enforcement against compounders differs from traditional Hatch-Waxman litigation. The automatic 30-month stay would not apply against compounders. Branded companies would need to prove entitlement to preliminary injunction, shifting litigation focus to damages.

art-5e91706c1ef8.title: FDA's Latest GLP-1 Crackdown: What Compounders and Telehealth Platforms Need to Know
art-5e91706c1ef8.summary: Venable analysis noting that unsafe compounding threatens the reputation of compounding done safely. Compounding is intended to be specialized to an individual patient, but it becomes a gray area when mass-produced and mass-distributed.

art-3461222426b0.title: APC CEO Statement on FDA's Announced Intention to Restrict GLP-1 APIs
art-3461222426b0.summary: Alliance for Pharmacy Compounding CEO Scott Brunner responds to FDA's GLP-1 API restriction announcement. Notes that over four years of compounded GLP-1 activity, FDA had not asserted that semaglutide or tirzepatide APIs used by drugmakers are superior to or different from those used by compounding pharmacies.

art-ed867f8432b0.title: APC Releases 2025-2026 Snapshot of Pharmacy Compounding in America
art-ed867f8432b0.summary: Alliance for Pharmacy Compounding publishes comprehensive industry survey covering compounding volumes, market trends, regulatory compliance challenges, and the shifting landscape for 503A and 503B facilities nationwide.

art-10f1ed57126a.title: APC Releases 14-Point Blueprint for Smarter Federal Regulation of Compounding
art-10f1ed57126a.summary: APC proposes a 14-point regulatory blueprint to streamline federal oversight of compounding while protecting patient access. Recommendations address FDA inspection processes, bulk drug substance policies, and interstate compounding frameworks.

art-3cae83215d91.title: Hims Cancels Plans to Sell Compounded GLP-1 Pill After FDA Backlash
art-3cae83215d91.summary: Hims abandoned plans to sell a compounded version of oral Wegovy following backlash from FDA Commissioner Makary, who threatened swift action against companies mass-marketing illegal copycat drugs. Novo Nordisk's testing found impurities of up to 86% in compounded injectable semaglutide and up to 75% in oral versions.

art-152b5e7e8a24.title: Novo Nordisk Sues Hims & Hers Over Compounded Obesity Drugs
art-152b5e7e8a24.summary: Novo Nordisk filed patent infringement lawsuit against Hims for US Patent 8,129,343 over compounded semaglutide products. Sought permanent ban on Hims selling compounded versions and recovery of damages. Later settled via partnership agreement in March 2026.

art-71160e84eb6e.title: U.S. Signals Crackdown on Compounded Weight-Loss Drugs
art-71160e84eb6e.summary: FDA announced it would restrict GLP-1 APIs in non-approved compounded drugs. On the same day, HHS General Counsel referred Hims to the DOJ for investigation of potential federal law violations. Hims shares tumbled on the news.

art-1e64eec3d7cc.title: Eli Lilly Warns Compounded GLP-1 Knockoffs May Be Dangerous
art-1e64eec3d7cc.summary: Eli Lilly issued open letter warning that compounded tirzepatide with vitamin B12 contains significant levels of an unknown impurity from a chemical reaction between the two substances. Also found bacterial contamination, high endotoxin levels, and other impurities in compounded knockoffs.

art-60bde3096c5d.title: FDA to Restrict Ingredients Used in Mass-Marketed Compounded GLP-1s, Crack Down on Misleading Ads
art-60bde3096c5d.summary: AJMC coverage of FDA's dual-pronged enforcement strategy: restricting GLP-1 APIs in mass-marketed compounded products while simultaneously cracking down on misleading direct-to-consumer advertising. Over the past six months, the agency sent thousands of warning letters.

art-7e1479a11f21.title: Oral Semaglutide and the GLP-1 Compounding Reckoning
art-7e1479a11f21.summary: Buchanan Ingersoll analysis tracing the arc from regulatory ambiguity to FDA enforcement, DOJ referrals, and the Novo Nordisk case against Hims. Examines how the oral semaglutide approval transformed the enforcement landscape for compounders.

art-70c6c86c5359.title: The Government May Soon Lift Restrictions on Some Peptide Treatments
art-70c6c86c5359.summary: NPR reports that because of FDA restrictions, patients have turned to unregulated overseas suppliers, presenting new safety dangers from potentially adulterated products. HHS has not provided a specific timeline for formal reclassification.

art-490254605b31.title: FDA Removes Certain Peptide Bulk Drug Substances from Category 2, Sets PCAC Review Dates
art-490254605b31.summary: Five peptides (AOD-9604, CJC-1295, ipamorelin acetate, thymosin alpha-1, Selank acetate) removed from Category 2 as of September 27, 2024 based on nominators' withdrawal. PCAC voted against inclusion of ipamorelin, CJC-1295, AOD-9604, and thymosin alpha-1 in the 503A bulks regulation.

art-21023dc5ee5c.title: The Peptide Landscape Is Shifting: What RFK Jr.'s Announcement Could Mean for Compounding
art-21023dc5ee5c.summary: Frier Levitt analysis of the implications of RFK Jr.'s peptide reclassification announcement for compounding pharmacies. Examines the gap between the policy announcement and formal regulatory action, and what pharmacies should do in the interim.

art-81bb50453504.title: Hims Faces Regulatory and Legal Risks in GLP-1 Business Expansion
art-81bb50453504.summary: Financial analysis of Hims regulatory exposure including SEC Division of Enforcement investigation into public statements and disclosures regarding compounded semaglutide and related business relationships.

art-d3fc05ff85b3.title: GLP-1 Receptor Agonists: Drug Litigation Overview and Trends
art-d3fc05ff85b3.summary: Foley and Lardner comprehensive overview of GLP-1 litigation landscape covering patent disputes, FDA enforcement actions, ITC proceedings, and state-level regulatory challenges facing compounders.

art-9def358a318a.title: Why the FDA Issued 30 Warning Letters to Telehealth Sites Marketing Compounded GLP-1s
art-9def358a318a.summary: Pharmaceutical Technology analysis of the specific violations cited in the March 2026 warning letters, including claims implying FDA approval, obscuring compounding sources, and using brand-adjacent naming conventions.

art-c7992b4c8916.title: Compounded GLP-1s: Filling a Medical Need But Posing What Dangers?
art-c7992b4c8916.summary: Health Law and Policy Brief analysis examining the tension between patient access and safety concerns in the compounded GLP-1 market, including cold-chain integrity vulnerabilities and fraud risks from counterfeit products.

art-0f2be73eb259.title: FDA Reaffirms Strict Regulations Amid GLP-1 Compounding Concerns
art-0f2be73eb259.summary: Drug Topics reports that high-volume, standardized formulation, and national distribution are now being treated as manufacturing rather than compounding. 503A pharmacies must remain patient-specific in both form and reality or face enforcement.

art-e064f614a70f.title: Compounded GLP-1 Drugs: Texas Judge Denies PI Motion, Plaintiff OFA Appeals
art-e064f614a70f.summary: U.S. District Court in Northern District of Texas ruled in favor of FDA, denying Outsourcing Facilities Association motion for preliminary injunction regarding tirzepatide compounding. OFA filed interlocutory appeal to the Fifth Circuit on March 10, 2025.

art-aa1d54f279a7.title: California Board of Pharmacy's New 'Essentially a Copy' Rules: What GLP-1 Compounders Need to Know
art-aa1d54f279a7.summary: California's new rules define a compounded drug as 'essentially a copy' if it contains the same API unless a change produces a 'clinically significant difference' verified and documented by the pharmacist. The Board has not issued formal guidance defining what constitutes sufficient evidence.

art-20b88c5b922d.title: California SB 282: New Compounding and Medical Spa Regulation
art-20b88c5b922d.summary: Introduced January 2026, California SB 282 would tighten state controls on drug compounding and create a new registration and enforcement framework for medical spas. Effective July 1, 2026, the bill would restrict compounding using bulk drug substances unless specified statutory requirements are met.

art-2b8ba19c90d8.title: Florida SB 860: A New Playbook for Weight-Loss Compounding
art-2b8ba19c90d8.summary: Florida SB 860 would specifically regulate compounded drugs for weight loss, requiring API sourcing from FDA-registered facilities inspected within two years, pharmaceutical-grade APIs, and independent quality-control testing. Filed December 10, 2025, pending committee action.

art-626da76eff37.title: State and Federal Legislative Changes Reshape Drug Compounding Regulation in 2026
art-626da76eff37.summary: Overview of recent state and federal legislative developments affecting compounding, including Florida SB 860/HB 877 and California Board of Pharmacy rule changes impacting interstate compounding.

art-f16026d10a8c.title: Ohio Board of Pharmacy Issues FAQs for GLP-1 Compounders
art-f16026d10a8c.summary: Ohio Board of Pharmacy guidance states providers should generally use FDA-approved GLP-1 drugs rather than compounded versions. Compounded copies of tirzepatide and semaglutide are not permitted when commercially available. APIs must be purchased from Ohio-licensed distributors.

art-7b6ca4bb5021.title: Arizona's Withdrawn GLP-1 Compounding Bill Signals Increased Scrutiny
art-7b6ca4bb5021.summary: Arizona HB 4036 was withdrawn before advancing but its provisions signal heightened legislative scrutiny of GLP-1 compounding. Would have required pharmaceutical-grade bulk drug substances from FDA-inspected facilities, quality control testing, two-year record retention, and advertising disclosures.

art-86a9b731a3c7.title: Indiana SB 282: Compounding Oversight and Medical Spa Regulation
art-86a9b731a3c7.summary: Indiana SB 282 would tighten bulk drug substance compounding, expand oversight and recordkeeping, and regulate medical spas through a new registration and enforcement regime. House-amended version passed with companion trailer bill HB 1277 to address amino acid language concerns.

art-0140df6c9309.title: Colorado SB 26-066: Compounding Restrictions That Could Limit GLP-1 Access
art-0140df6c9309.summary: Colorado SB 26-066 scheduled for hearing in Senate Health and Human Services Committee. Although framed as targeting counterfeit drugs, the bill would make it harder for licensed pharmacies to provide compounded GLP-1 medications that many patients rely on.

art-cc6779fbd332.title: The GLP-1 Drug Boom Has a Dangerous Gray Market: Arizona Lawmakers Should Step In
art-cc6779fbd332.summary: Investigative report on Arizona's unregulated GLP-1 compounding market and the legislative response. Documents quality and safety concerns as well as the debate between patient access and regulatory oversight at the state level.

art-c1c83575276e.title: Despite FDA Crackdown, Unapproved GLP-1s Still Threaten the Industry
art-c1c83575276e.summary: Despite thousands of warning letters, compounded semaglutide prescriptions continued to increase. About 80% of compounded GLP-1 prescriptions now include supplemental ingredients to argue they aren't exact copies.

art-853ac744d974.title: 6 Recent Updates on GLP-1 Drugs
art-853ac744d974.summary: Becker's roundup of the latest GLP-1 developments including FDA enforcement actions, Novo Nordisk citizen petition, and compounding deadline updates.

art-623967b69cb2.title: Peptide Therapy in 2026: From Grey Zone to Guardrails
art-623967b69cb2.summary: Analysis of the evolving regulatory landscape for peptide therapy, examining how providers should navigate the gap between announced policy intentions and formal regulatory changes.

art-352711238626.title: The FDA Is Targeting Telehealth Marketing of GLP-1 Drugs. Who's Prescribing Them?
art-352711238626.summary: STAT investigation into the prescribers behind telehealth companies receiving FDA warning letters over compounded GLP-1 marketing violations.

art-8d64400357eb.title: From Crackdown to Collaboration: FDA Warning Letters and the Hims-Novo Nordisk Deal Signal a New Era
art-8d64400357eb.summary: Legal analysis of how the simultaneous FDA enforcement crackdown and Hims-Novo partnership may reshape the compounded GLP-1 landscape for telehealth companies and compounding pharmacies.

art-ec51a4bae257.title: The FDA Peptide Reclassification: What Actually Changed in 2026
art-ec51a4bae257.summary: Fact-check analysis: no Federal Register notice has been issued broadly removing peptides from Category 2. Official changes expected through formal administrative channels in coming weeks.

art-63a0dcf545b5.title: Hims & Hers Announces Strategic Shift: Partners with Novo Nordisk, Drops Compounded GLP-1 Marketing
art-63a0dcf545b5.summary: Hims & Hers will offer Novo Nordisk's branded Ozempic and Wegovy on its platform while ceasing advertising of compounded GLP-1 drugs. Novo Nordisk drops patent infringement lawsuit. Shares surged 36%.

art-6cefb237e13b.title: Novo and Hims Make Nice, Striking Deal to Sell Ozempic, Wegovy on Hims' Telehealth Platform
art-6cefb237e13b.summary: Detailed analysis of the Novo-Hims partnership signaling a new era where branded and compounded GLP-1s may coexist through regulated channels rather than litigation.

art-8bdce186fe13.title: FDA Moves to Restrict Mass-Marketed Compounded GLP-1 Products
art-8bdce186fe13.summary: FDA announced action to restrict GLP-1 APIs in non-FDA-approved compounded drugs being mass-marketed as alternatives. Violators face seizure or injunction without further notice.

art-e6f22b1a3d15.title: FDA Warns 30 Telehealth Companies Against Illegal Marketing of Compounded GLP-1s
art-e6f22b1a3d15.summary: FDA issued 30 warning letters to telehealth companies for false or misleading claims about compounded GLP-1 products. Letters require corrective steps within 15 working days or risk seizure and injunction.

art-5448c7e2bfdd.title: FDA Ramps Up Crackdown on GLP-1 Drug Compounding with Fresh Batch of 30 Warning Letters
art-5448c7e2bfdd.summary: Second batch of warning letters targeting companies like Lovely Meds, Hello Cake, and MEDVi. Violations include implying sameness with FDA-approved products and obscuring product sourcing.

art-2d4700b28e18.title: RFK Jr, Peptides & FDA Category 2: What's Really Changing?
art-2d4700b28e18.summary: Legal analysis clarifying that as of March 2026, no formal FDA rule has changed. The 14 peptides remain Category 2 substances until the FDA formally updates the list through administrative channels.

art-0fc2e2d5c93d.title: RFK Jr. Announces Plan to Move 14 Peptides from Category 2 Back to Category 1
art-0fc2e2d5c93d.summary: HHS Secretary RFK Jr. announced on Feb 27, 2026 that approximately 14 of 19 peptides on the FDA Category 2 restricted list will be moved back to Category 1, restoring access through licensed compounding pharmacies. Includes BPC-157, Thymosin Alpha-1, AOD-9604, Semax, Selank, KPV, and MOTS-C.

art-db3830b69054.title: Don't Use Drug Products Intended to Be Sterile from This Houston Compounding Pharmacy: FDA
art-db3830b69054.summary: FDA warned healthcare providers and consumers not to use sterile drug products from a Houston compounding pharmacy due to quality and safety concerns.

art-e1a9d4607293.title: FDA Commissioner Makary Declares Upcoming Crackdown on GLP-1 Claims, Importation
art-e1a9d4607293.summary: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary stated FDA will focus on importation of unapproved APIs, signaling increased inspection activity, border restrictions, or both for compounding pharmacies.

art-1f665d807fbd.title: FDA in Flux — February 2026 Newsletter
art-1f665d807fbd.summary: Monthly overview of FDA regulatory developments including compounding enforcement, peptide reclassification discussions, and telehealth advertising crackdowns.

art-26c244a47803.title: Pennsylvania Compounding Pharmacy Earns Another FDA Warning Letter
art-26c244a47803.summary: Boothwyn Pharmacy, LLC cited for sterility failures, subpotent injectable and ophthalmic drugs, and insanitary production conditions.

art-d203080e1474.title: State Compounding Legislation: Federal Preemption, State Limits and Patient Impact
art-d203080e1474.summary: Analysis of state-level compounding legislation, including Florida SB 860 and California Board of Pharmacy rules redefining 'essentially a copy' with new API sourcing requirements.

art-f004d5ada8c9.title: FDA's Overreach on Compounded Peptides: Legal Battles and How Clinics Can Push Back
art-f004d5ada8c9.summary: Analysis of legal challenges to the FDA's 2023 decision to move 19 peptides to Category 2, and strategies for clinics navigating the current regulatory uncertainty.

art-052a673a4fa2.title: Thinking About Starting a 503B Outsourcing Facility? Here's What You Need to Know
art-052a673a4fa2.summary: Comprehensive regulatory guide for launching a 503B outsourcing facility, covering FDA registration, cGMP requirements, inspection readiness, and the evolving enforcement landscape.

art-6be14a504f08.title: Compounding Pharmacy Sues Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk over GLP-1 Access
art-6be14a504f08.summary: Compounding pharmacy files suit against pharmaceutical giants over GLP-1 access restrictions, arguing the companies are using patent litigation to block legitimate compounding activity.

art-47e7bf840e7a.title: FDA Updates Hospital Pharmacy Compounding Rules to Address Potential Shortages
art-47e7bf840e7a.summary: FDA updated guidance to allow small compounding pharmacies to supply hospitals during drug shortages, expanding the role of 503A pharmacies in the supply chain.

art-9f8e96c21237.title: FDA Allowing Small Compounding Pharmacies to Supply Hospitals
art-9f8e96c21237.summary: FDA expanded the role of 503A compounding pharmacies to help address hospital drug shortage needs through updated guidance.

art-73d26271e0ab.title: Navigating the FDA's Final Interim Policy on Compounding with Bulk Drug Substances
art-73d26271e0ab.summary: Detailed guide for 503A pharmacies and 503B outsourcing facilities on navigating the FDA's final interim policy, including compliance requirements, Category 1/2 distinctions, and enforcement discretion.

art-facaafcb3a7b.title: Deep Dive: Regulatory Status of Popular Compounded Peptides
art-facaafcb3a7b.summary: Comprehensive legal analysis of the regulatory status of each commonly compounded peptide, including BPC-157, TB-500, and Thymosin Alpha-1 under current FDA framework.

art-f74b8c89acba.title: Best GLP-1 Telehealth Platforms (2026): Launch & Scale D2C Programs
art-f74b8c89acba.summary: Comprehensive comparison of white-label telehealth platforms for GLP-1 programs including Bask Health, Wheel, and CareValidate with launch timelines of 2-8 weeks.

art-38887c9ab4c7.title: FDA Probes Compounding Pharmacies over Quality Concerns
art-38887c9ab4c7.summary: FDA plans to gather information from 250 compounding outsourcing pharmacies amid safety and quality concerns, surveying facilities about market viability, compliance, and quality production.

art-ff0d7b05face.title: FDA Enforcement Review: Looking Back at 2025 and Bracing for Continued Unpredictability in 2026
art-ff0d7b05face.summary: Comprehensive review of FDA enforcement trends, including the unprecedented wave of advertising enforcement letters and the evolving approach to compounding oversight under Commissioner Makary.

art-1f91702f4b4e.title: 2025 Year in Review: FDA Drug and Device Advertising and Promotion Enforcement
art-1f91702f4b4e.summary: Total enforcement letters for pharmaceutical advertising violations marked the highest annual total in nearly 25 years, driven heavily by compounded GLP-1 and telehealth enforcement.

art-3e490ef3acf8.title: Navigating 503B Outsourcing Trends and 2026 Outlook
art-3e490ef3acf8.summary: 503B facility outlook: more than half have not been inspected since the pandemic. An inspection wave is likely in 2026. FDA enforcement discretion for semaglutide and tirzepatide under final review.

art-b2edfb00b7fc.title: FDA Takes Aim at Drug Ads: What It Means for Compounding Pharmacies, Medspas and Telehealth Companies
art-b2edfb00b7fc.summary: Analysis of FDA's enforcement initiative against misleading advertising in compounded drugs, including claims about 'generic' or 'clinically proven' versions of approved GLP-1 therapies.

art-8bef29b3350a.title: Drug Approvals to Watch in 2026
art-8bef29b3350a.summary: Orforglipron, a once-daily oral GLP-1 receptor agonist, is under FDA priority review with a decision expected in March 2026. Could reshape the compounding landscape further.

art-b4bc54b074e2.title: FDA Approves First GLP-1 Pill for Obesity from Novo Nordisk
art-b4bc54b074e2.summary: FDA approved Novo Nordisk's 25 mg oral Wegovy, the first FDA-approved oral GLP-1 therapy for chronic weight management in adults. Major milestone that may further impact the compounding landscape.

art-aaf6cb34bae0.title: Bask Health: Pioneering Telehealth and Transforming Access to GLP-1 Medications
art-aaf6cb34bae0.summary: Bask Health's white-label platform combines EMR, e-prescribing, pharmacy fulfillment, and nationwide clinician network for GLP-1, TRT, ED, and peptide programs across all 50 states.

art-d5b32277a438.title: Congress Introduces SAFE Drugs Act of 2025, Expanding FDA Oversight of Compounded GLP-1 Drugs and Telehealth
art-d5b32277a438.summary: H.R. 6509 introduced Dec 9, 2025. Requires pre-compounding inspections for 503B facilities, biennial re-inspections for high-volume compounders, reporting for interstate shipments exceeding 20 Rx/drug, and codifies 'essentially a copy' definition.

art-1a78169f2a85.title: SAFE Drugs Act of 2025 — Full Text (H.R. 6509)
art-1a78169f2a85.summary: Full text of the SAFE Drugs Act proposing expanded FDA oversight of compounding pharmacies, outsourcing facilities, and telehealth distribution models for compounded drugs.

art-72bfe1f0a8c4.title: Federal Register: Human Drug Compounding Under Sections 503A and 503B — Proposed Collection Request
art-72bfe1f0a8c4.summary: FDA proposes to gather information from 250 compounding outsourcing facilities on challenges related to market viability, compliance, quality production, and FDA interactions.

art-9bc7fbfd24cc.title: U.S. House Introduces Bill to Expand Compounding During Drug Shortages
art-9bc7fbfd24cc.summary: Proposed legislation to expand compounding during drug shortages, provide clarity on bulk substance USP/NF monograph definitions, and reform Section 503A's memorandum of understanding provisions.

art-bb0e455de5c2.title: FY 2026 Outsourcing Facility Registration and Fee Information
art-bb0e455de5c2.summary: FY 2026 registration period for outsourcing facilities runs Oct 1, 2025 through Dec 31, 2026. Fee rates published in Federal Register on July 30, 2025.

art-6039e0278661.title: FDA, HHS Taking Action Against Telehealth's Compounded Drug Advertising
art-6039e0278661.summary: FDA and HHS launched an enforcement initiative targeting misleading advertising in the compounded drug space, flagging claims implying equivalence to FDA-approved products.

art-247f1eabe120.title: Companies Find Ways Around End of GLP-1 Shortage
art-247f1eabe120.summary: Despite shortages being resolved, compounders continued operations by adding supplemental ingredients or altering dosage forms to argue products aren't 'essentially copies' of FDA-approved drugs.

art-6f731d179f0e.title: Semaglutide Shortage Resolved — What It Means for Compounders
art-6f731d179f0e.summary: FDA declared semaglutide shortage resolved on Feb 21, 2025. 503A pharmacies had until April 22, 2025 to cease compounding. 503B outsourcing facilities until May 22, 2025.

art-ed3e02f85d79.title: FDA Clarifies Policies for Compounders as National GLP-1 Supply Begins to Stabilize
art-ed3e02f85d79.summary: FDA formally clarified enforcement discretion timelines: 503A pharmacies must cease compounding semaglutide by April 22, 2025. 503B outsourcing facilities by May 22, 2025.

art-fa4e94fe1af6.title: FDA Releases Revised Guidance for Compounding Pharmacies on Bulk Drug Substances
art-fa4e94fe1af6.summary: Under revised guidance, 503A pharmacies can compound Category 1 substances but cannot compound substances without USP monographs (like most peptides) until further review. 503B facilities limited to Category 1 bulk list or FDA-approved drugs in shortage.

art-28ff21837db3.title: Registered Outsourcing Facilities
art-28ff21837db3.summary: Discover FDA's comprehensive list of registered outsourcing facilities engaged in human drug compounding. Get the latest updates, compliance guidelines, and resources to ensure safe, high-quality compounded medications. Explore regulations and stay informed with verified facility details.

art-02ed3a013ca0.title: Bulk Drug Substances Used in Compounding
art-02ed3a013ca0.summary: Compounders sometimes produce drugs using bulk drug substances or active pharmaceutical ingredients. Because compounding from these substances presents risks to patients, sections 503A and 503B of the FD&C Act place limits on the bulk drug substances that can be used in compounding.

art-0d45bd974ac0.title: FDA Approves Fourth Product Under National Priority Voucher Program, Higher Dose Semaglutide
art-0d45bd974ac0.summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved a new higher dose (7.2 mg) of Wegovy (semaglutide) injection for weight loss and long-term maintenance of weight loss for certain adult patients.

art-df9ca0b317d8.title: FDA Warns 30 Telehealth Companies Against Illegal Marketing of Compounded GLP-1s
art-df9ca0b317d8.summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced the issuance of 30 warning letters to telehealth companies for making false or misleading claims regarding compounded GLP-1 products offered on their websites.

art-be4587e9784f.title: FDA Intends to Take Action Against Non-FDA-Approved GLP-1 Drugs
art-be4587e9784f.summary: Today, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration is announcing its intent to take decisive steps to restrict GLP-1 active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) intended for use in non-FDA-approved compounded drugs that are being mass-marketed by companies

art-6230655ba999.title: Compounders on Capitol HillAPC’s annual D.C. fly-in is your best chance all year to meet with federal legislators on their turf. Share your concerns and brief them on the issues facing your compoundin

art-e15cba45f357.title: EduConOur annual virtual education conference focuses on hot topics and ethical compounding.

art-a773768a5ae8.title: Owner SummitA one-of-a-kind learning, planning, and networking especially for compounding pharmacy leaders. Two days of meaty presentations and conversations focused on helping you run your business b

art-fd728d79e524.title: Federal AdvocacyLobbying on Capitol Hill to assure that legislation elevates and preserves pure ingredient compounding

art-12d7d6dd1a48.title: State Level ResourcesAPC has three members-only resources covering state-level compounding issues. Plus, find what we are working on.

art-ffee2a56d4af.title: CompPACHelp elect candidates who think like you do about pharmacy compounding.

art-52bceee95e43.title: APC Position StatementsFrom time to time, APC makes its position clear on critical issues related to compounding.

art-518673f844f3.title: Ethical CompoundingThere’s an old saying, “You can’t talk your way out of a problem you behaved your way into.”

art-2ae06452bedf.title: Compounding Foundations CourseThe Ethical, Legal, and Regulatory Foundations of Pharmacy Compounding

art-520d2c1475fe.title: Compounding Best PracticesDefined, science-based best practice guidelines for compounding certain medications and for other critical processes.
